Agribusiness Consulting · Accra / Northern Ghana & West Africa

Turning Agriculture into Structured, Profitable Business Systems

We solve the business, operations, reporting, and strategy problems that prevent agribusinesses from growing — across Ghana and West Africa.

Book a Discovery Call View Our Services
The Problem We Solve

Ghana's agriculture sector generates billions — yet most agribusinesses operate without formal systems.

The result is poor performance visibility, weak funding readiness, and stunted growth. AgriValue Advisory Consult exists to close that gap. We do not provide farming advice. We solve the business and operational problems behind the growth ceiling.

Our Flagship Offers

Three Done-For-You Consulting Offers

Each offer bundles multiple disciplines into a single client outcome — eliminating confusion, accelerating decisions, and delivering measurable results.

Offer 01

The Agri-Performance Diagnostic™

"Know exactly where you are losing money — fixed in 3 weeks."

GHS 12,000 – GHS 35,000
15–21 working days
  • Full operations review & workflow assessment
  • Process gap and bottleneck analysis
  • KPI dashboard setup (Excel / Power BI)
  • 30-page diagnostic report
  • 3-month performance monitoring template
Offer 02 Most Popular

The Funding-Ready Pack™

"A bank- or donor-ready proposal and a team trained to implement it."

GHS 8,000 – GHS 20,000
10–20 working days
  • Concept note & funder alignment
  • Full proposal with logical framework
  • M&E framework & indicator design
  • Budget structure & cost justification
  • 1-day project team capacity training
Offer 03

The Market Access Booster™

"A new buyer pipeline and a sales-ready team — within 60 days."

GHS 15,000 – GHS 40,000
30–45 working days
  • Business & revenue model redesign
  • Market opportunity & buyer landscape analysis
  • 30/60/90-day strategic growth roadmap
  • 2-day market access & sales training
  • Buyer pitch deck & positioning brief
What We Help Solve

The Business Problems Behind the Growth Ceiling

Agriculture often struggles not because of poor effort, but because of weak systems and unclear structure.

Workflows & Execution

  • Inefficient workflows that waste time, resources, and create costly delays
  • Execution bottlenecks — good ideas stuck at the idea stage, projects never implemented

Coordination & Structure

  • Weak coordination — teams working in silos with no shared visibility or accountability
  • Low operational structure — no documented processes, responsibilities, or performance systems

Reporting & Data

  • Poor reporting visibility — no dashboards, no KPIs, no reliable data for management decisions
  • Scattered data collected but never structured or used to drive decision-making

Growth & Funding

  • Unclear growth direction — founders unsure of next steps, market positioning, or how to scale
  • Underdeveloped project ideas unable to attract funding due to weak documentation

Our Methodology

How We Work — The DADIT Framework

A structured five-step consulting approach that moves from problem clarity to measurable performance.

D

Diagnose

Assess the current challenge, structure, or opportunity in context.

A

Analyse

Identify root causes, gaps, and priority improvement areas.

D

Design

Create practical solutions, frameworks, and clear recommendations.

I

Implement

Support clients in putting structure into action with clear milestones.

T

Track

Improve visibility and support ongoing performance monitoring.

Our Purpose

Vision & Mission

Vision

"To become the leading agribusiness systems and strategy advisory firm in Ghana and West Africa — known for transforming agriculture organisations from informal, fragmented operations into structured, profitable, and scalable business systems."

Mission

"To provide practical, locally rooted, and data-driven agribusiness advisory services that help agriculture organisations improve performance, attract funding, and scale sustainably."

The Bigger Picture

Agriculture is the backbone of Ghana's economy and the livelihood of over 50% of its population. Its transformation will not happen through farming advice alone.

It will happen when the business systems, reporting infrastructure, market access capabilities, and funding readiness of agriculture organisations reach international standards. That is what AgriValue Advisory Consult is building — one client at a time, starting in Tamale, scaling across Ghana, and eventually serving the agriculture sectors of West Africa's most productive economies.

Leadership

Meet the Founder

SA
Sule Abdul Rahman
Founder & Principal Consultant

Sule didn't study agriculture from a classroom — he grew up on a farm. Raised in Bando, near Elmina, he lost his father at an early age and faced financial hardship so severe he burned charcoal to fund his own education. Instead of yielding, he turned every setback into fuel. He studied agriculture formally at secondary and college level, completed his national service with CSIR-SARI in Nyankpala, then spent 7 years as a Technical Field Specialist with Masara N'Arziki Farmers Association, followed by 3 years as a Commercial Business Partner Coordinator with Agricultural Labour Services Agency (ALSA) — not as an observer, but as someone doing the work.

That field experience grew into something sharper. Armed with a Bachelor of Business Administration from Valley View University and certification as a Certified Business Analyst, Sule moved into the systems and strategy side of agriculture — training technical staff and frontline teams for organisations including Babator Farming Company, building operational capacity where it was needed most. AgriValue Advisory Consult is the direct product of that journey: two decades of agriculture, business analysis, and people development, combined into one focused consulting practice.
